text: The NBA will announce this season's MVP winner on Sunday night, the day before two of the finalists, the Thunder's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Spurs' Victor Wembanyama, meet in Game 1 of the Western Conference finals.
title: NBA to unveil MVP on Sunday, day before Thunder-Spurs series

text: Thunder star Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has won his second consecutive NBA Most Valuable Player award, becoming the 14th player in league history to win back-to-back MVPs, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania.
title: Sources: Thunder's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander repeats as NBA MVP

text: Shams Charania joins "SportsCenter" to break the news that Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has won his second consecutive MVP award.
title: Shams: SGA to be named MVP for 2nd straight season

text: San Antonio Spurs (62-20, second in the Western Conference) vs. Oklahoma City Thunder (64-18, first in the Western Conference)
title: Wembanyama and the Spurs visit Oklahoma City to start Western Conference finals

text: The PWHL is bringing women's hockey to the San Francisco Bay Area by choosing San Jose, California, as its fourth and final market during the league's latest round of expansion, according to multiple reports.
title: Reports: PWHL chooses San Jose as its 4th expansion market

text: With Game 6 out of reach, Anthony Edwards walked over to the Spurs bench to "give them their respect" after the Timberwolves fell short in the playoffs for the third straight season.
title: Edwards unsure how Wolves can get over hump after loss to Spurs

title: NBA championship and Finals MVP odds: Pistons 30-1 to win championship
